the developer "Ubisoft" has discontinued support for a videogame "The Crew" and completely removed/disallowed players from continuing to enjoy a product that they've payed for.

Nintendo", with it's releas of a new console "switch 2", I belive has violated cunsumer laws by not allowing modifications to it's software or phisical hardware that they've sold through "bricking" (which is remotly locking and/or diabeling software and hardware included with the switch 2).

paragraphs.text: As a Polish, as well as an EU citizen, in my opinion, lawful protection of human and consumer rights should be manditory. I wasn't prepared to how fast the internet would develop, nobody was. I was even more unprepared to how this speed got exploited for corporate profit. From constantly increasing costs of monthly subsciptions, like Netflix or Disney+; Unsubscription fees, done by Adobe (I still can't belive it's presumably allowed and it shouldn't); All the way up to predatory non-skippable Ad placements, not just on streaming services (That still require a monthly subscription, like amazon prime video) but also full-priced videogames (NBA2K 21). Those are only a few greavences I, as well as meny others may, have about subscriptions. However, I belive many people would agree that, once a good is purchased, It should belong to a person who purchased it. Including digital goods. Products, usually in a form of software with a one time fee such as: - An operating system (Especially one included with hardware) - image/audio/video editing software - Videogames the moment they are purchased, should belong to the consumer without question, for ever. And said consumer should have the right to modify these programs however they like without any repercussion from a company that sold it (the most severe punishment should only be a loss of warrenty) As much as I would like this to be the case, it's not currently. The developer "Ubisoft" has discontinued support for a videogame "The Crew" and completely removed/disallowed players from continuing to enjoy a product that they've payed for. Doe to this, a European Citizens' Initiative known as "Stop Destroying Videogames" has garnerd over 1.3 milion signatures, In hopes of protecting consumer right to own the products that have been payed for, along side the art that videogames represent. A company "Nintendo", with it's releas of a new console "switch 2", I belive has violated cunsumer laws by not allowing modifications to it's software or phisical hardware that they've sold through "bricking" (which is remotly locking and/or diabeling software and hardware included with the switch 2). This sparked a lawsuit between the Nintendo company and a Brazilian consumer protection agency. As a remote disabaling of software quite literally shouldn't be allowed. (At worst, ONLY warrenty should be lost) I hope this put a spotlight on the most severe issues that could and do affect me diractly as a consumer. My wish is that these concerns, along side concerns of other consumers like me, are reviewed positively. Thank you for your time. (Appologies for spelling mistakes, I found it easyer to write in english, as I've found out a lot of this information in this language)
